I should also consider other possibilities, like cellulitis, but that's usually caused by a bacterial infection and would present with more systemic symptoms like fever.
Eczema is another option, but it's more chronic and not necessarily triggered by an external application.
Given the timing of the symptoms after using Neosporin, an allergic reaction seems more likely.

So, the most probable diagnosis is allergic contact dermatitis caused by Neosporin, complicated by her underlying venous insufficiency which made her legs more susceptible to inflammation and fluid retention.
